viralFormats: Rapid-fire narration with bold text overlays and dynamic visuals
Greenscreen reactions/commentary on hypothetical prompts
Short animated sequences depicting the 'what if' scenario
POV acting out an immediate consequence of the hypothetical
Comparison charts or split screens showing 'before vs. after' the hypothetical
hooks: What if (shocking scenario)...?
Imagine a world where (unusual condition)...
This 'what if' will blow your mind...
Could YOU survive if (extreme event)...?
The craziest 'what if' question you'll hear today...
videoStructures: Hook -> Introduce hypothetical -> Brief visual/audio exploration of scenario -> Implied consequence/open question
Hook -> State 'what if' -> Act out a short, immediate reaction/outcome -> Punchline/cliffhanger
Hook -> Present 'normal' situation -> Twist with 'what if' -> Rapid-fire visual examples of new reality -> Call to action to comment
popularMusic: Fast-paced, high-energy background music for quick cuts
Sound effects for emphasis (whooshes, dings, dramatic stings)

viralFormats: Fast-cut montages of mishaps
POV scenarios with unexpected, often awkward, endings
Green screen content integrating chaotic elements into mundane scenes

hooks: You won't believe what happened next...
This is your sign to NOT try this at home.
My brain at 3 AM, visualized:
POV: when [relatable chaotic situation]
Watch till the end, it gets so much worse.
I tried this so you don't have to (and now I regret it).
videoStructures: Setup-Punchline: Quick intro to a situation, followed by an immediate chaotic resolution.
POV Scenario: Viewer is placed into a first-person perspective of an unfolding chaotic event.
Challenge/Experiment Gone Wrong: Show the intent, the execution, and the inevitable chaotic failure.
Rapid-fire Vignettes: A series of short, unrelated chaotic moments tied together by a specific sound or theme.
Anti-tutorial: Deliberately doing something wrong or inefficiently for comedic, chaotic effect.
popularMusic: Sound effects: Cartoon boings, record scratches, Wilhelm scream, dramatic stingers
Meme audios: Sped-up viral sounds, 'Oh no oh no oh no no no no', 'It's a me, Mario!'
Dramatic build-up music leading to an abrupt, chaotic drop
Upbeat, slightly dissonant background tracks that imply impending chaos
Nostalgic children's show themes remixed with an edgy, chaotic twist
